name: Randy Couture
winProfile: Big signature wins, lighter HW volume

qualityWinLedger.context: UFC light heavyweight champion and elite rival.
UFC heavyweight champion, but softer heavyweight-era context.
Dangerous heavyweight contender after Cro Cop upset.
Elite contender, close first fight context.
Rubber match win with injury/cut rivalry context.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Liddell losses were recovered by heavyweight title comeback. Brock closes late UFC elite-prime window.

lossContextEvents.context: Official title loss; Barnett’s failed drug test remains contextual but does not erase the result.
Doctor stoppage from an eyelid cut; technical-result context retained.
Couture remained championship-level and fought Liddell again.
Couture later returned to win the UFC heavyweight title.

shortCase: Couture is the chaos-era championship case: heavyweight gold, light heavyweight gold, huge title moments, and a resume that looks messy because he kept taking dangerous fights.
bestArgument: Couture's case starts with championship chaos: heavyweight gold, light heavyweight gold, huge upset wins, and title value across multiple eras.
counterArgument: The case against Couture is the record. He has more losses than the cleaner GOAT candidates, so his ranking depends on how much credit you give for title risk and era context.
whyRankedHere: Couture lands here because his UFC-only case is loaded with championship moments across heavyweight and light heavyweight. The record is messy, but his title wins, two-division relevance, and era-spanning opponent list carry real all-time weight.
whyNotHigher: He does not climb higher because the loss penalty and prime-dominance profile are much messier than the cleaner GOAT cases. His greatness is championship value and risk-taking, not spotless dominance.
finalTakeaway: Couture is the championship-chaos legend: not clean, not smooth, but too decorated across UFC title history to rank like a normal flawed contender.
